 {"id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","title":"[PACK] featureflags.unleash - Unleash Pack","description":"# Unleash Feature Flags Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Unleash (open-source feature management) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### unleash CLI
- `unleash features delete`
- `unleash projects delete`
- `unleash environments delete`
- `unleash strategies delete`

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE` to Unleash API
- `DELETE /api/admin/features/`
- `DELETE /api/admin/projects/`

## Safe Patterns
- `unleash features list`
- `unleash features get`
- `unleash projects list`
- GET API requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: featureflags.* (git_safety_guard-ekc)
- Keywords: unleash
- Note: Open-source, Unleash Edge for edge deployment

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for feature deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for project deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for API patterns
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/featureflags_unleash.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature deletion blocked
- [ ] Project deletion blocked
- [ ] List/get operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:03.322540015-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:53:55.51833458-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:53:55.51833458-05:00","close_reason":"Pack already implemented and tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.349833874-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.394208715-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.435565309-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.50523323-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.555090858-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.599663973-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.403861985-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.451398195-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ltou","title":"[CHECKLIST] Pack Implementation Completion Validation","description":"# Pack Implementation Completion Validation

## Purpose
This checklist MUST be completed before any pack is considered 'done'. Use this to validate each pack implementation.

## Pre-Merge Checklist

### Code Quality
- [ ] Pack follows existing code patterns (see `src/packs/database/postgresql.rs` as reference)
- [ ] All patterns use appropriate regex complexity (no catastrophic backtracking)
- [ ] Keywords are minimal and specific
- [ ] No duplicate patterns with other packs
- [ ] Code passes `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo check`

### Destructive Pattern Coverage
- [ ] All known destructive commands are covered
- [ ] Each pattern has a clear, specific reason string
- [ ] Patterns don't over-match (too broad)
- [ ] Patterns don't under-match (miss variations)

### Safe Pattern Coverage  
- [ ] Common safe commands are explicitly allowed
- [ ] Safe patterns take precedence correctly
- [ ] Read-only operations are not blocked

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Test file exists: `src/packs/<category>/<pack>.rs` (in #[cfg(test)] module)
- [ ] Each destructive pattern has at least one test
- [ ] Each safe pattern has at least one test
- [ ] Edge cases tested (quotes, special chars, empty input)
- [ ] Test coverage >= 90% for pack code

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Test file exists: `scripts/e2e_tests/<pack>.txt`
- [ ] Real-world command examples included
- [ ] Both block and allow cases tested
- [ ] E2E tests pass locally

### Documentation
- [ ] Pack added to README pack list
- [ ] Reference doc created: `docs/packs/<category>/<pack>.md`
- [ ] Configuration examples included
- [ ] Common issues documented

### Performance
- [ ] Pack evaluation < 500µs (benchmark)
- [ ] No regex patterns flagged as slow
- [ ] Total pattern count reasonable (< 50)

### Review
- [ ] Code review completed
- [ ] Test review completed
- [ ] Documentation review completed

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] CI passes on main branch
- [ ] No performance regression in nightly benchmarks
- [ ] Monitor for user-reported issues (1 week)

## Sign-Off
Pack implementation approved by: ________________
Date: ________________","notes":"Add to checklist:
- Verify pack e2e runs via shared harness with --verbose JSON logs and artifacts are stored for diffing.
- Confirm per-pack allow/deny cases appear in log summary with pack_id/pattern_name.
- Confirm perf budget checks (git_safety_guard-qxc7) and CI integration (git_safety_guard-6ozg) ran for the pack.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:01.230340756-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:48:25.332577307-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:48:25.332577307-05:00","close_reason":"Added pack implementation completion checklist doc and linked from pack task template"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ly4","title":"[TASK] Create E2E test script framework","description":"# E2E Test Script Framework

## Scope
Create a framework for end-to-end testing of packs using real dcg invocations.

## Deliverables

### 1. E2E Test Runner Script
Create `scripts/e2e_pack_test.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test framework for dcg packs
# Usage: ./e2e_pack_test.sh <pack_name> [--verbose]

set -euo pipefail

# Logging setup
LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
log_debug() { [[ $LOG_LEVEL == DEBUG ]] && echo "[DEBUG] $(date -Iseconds) $*" >&2; }
log_info()  { echo "[INFO]  $(date -Iseconds) $*" >&2; }
log_error() { echo "[ERROR] $(date -Iseconds) $*" >&2; }

# Test assertion functions
assert_blocked() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local reason="$2"
    log_debug "Testing that command is blocked: $cmd"
    # ... implementation
}

assert_allowed() {
    local cmd="$1"
    log_debug "Testing that command is allowed: $cmd"
    # ... implementation
}

# Results tracking
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0
```

### 2. Pack-Specific Test Files
Template for `scripts/e2e_tests/<pack_name>.txt`:
```
# Format: EXPECT_BLOCKED|EXPECT_ALLOWED command
# Lines starting with # are comments

# Destructive patterns
EXPECT_BLOCKED vault secrets disable secret-engine
EXPECT_BLOCKED vault kv delete secret/path

# Safe patterns  
EXPECT_ALLOWED vault read secret/path
EXPECT_ALLOWED vault list secret/
```

### 3. CI Integration
Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:
- Run E2E tests for all packs
- Parallel execution where possible
- Detailed failure reporting
- Test timing metrics

## Logging Requirements
- Each test case logs: command, expected result, actual result, timing
- Summary at end: total tests, passed, failed, timing
- Failed tests show full dcg output for debugging
- JSON output mode for CI parsing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E runner script implemented
- [ ] Test file format documented
- [ ] At least 3 example pack test files created
- [ ] CI workflow updated
- [ ] Verbose mode shows detailed debugging info","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:29.291067328-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:20:37.35871618-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T16:20:37.35871618-05:00","close_reason":"Obsolete: e2e framework already implemented as scripts/e2e_test.sh; keeping this open would duplicate harnesses. See closing comment for full rationale.","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ly4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Closing as obsolete/redundant: the requested E2E test *framework* already exists in-repo and is strictly better than what this bead proposes.

What this bead asked for (deliverables) vs what we already have:

1) "E2E runner script" (proposed: `scripts/e2e_pack_test.sh`)
- Already implemented as `scripts/e2e_test.sh`.
- It supports:
  - `--binary` to target a specific build
  - `--verbose` with per-test IDs + per-test timing
  - `--json` machine-readable output
  - `--artifacts DIR` to store stdout/stderr captures on failure
  - hermetic test env (isolated HOME/XDG config so local config doesn't leak into tests)
  - a large, real-world suite (currently 211 tests passing in baseline run)

2) "Pack-specific test files" / "test file format documented"
- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` currently stores its corpus inline, but the runner already has the core ergonomics (logging, timing, artifacts, JSON mode) this bead was meant to establish.
- If we want an externalized corpus format (YAML/text fixtures), that work should build on `scripts/e2e_test.sh` rather than creating a parallel runner.

3) "CI integration"
- The existing script is designed for CI use (deterministic, JSON mode, artifacts directory).
- CI wiring/parallelization is better handled in the CI-specific beads, not by creating a second runner.

Why closing (not merely downgrading priority):
- Keeping this open encourages duplicate implementations (`e2e_pack_test.sh` vs `e2e_test.sh`) and diverging test harnesses.
- Duplicate runners are a real maintenance hazard and tend to drift, which increases false positives/negatives in CI and wastes time.
- It also conflicts with the repo’s "no file proliferation" spirit: extending the existing runner is the pragmatic path.

Follow-on mapping (where the remaining intent lives now):
- If we want per-pack scenario definitions / external corpuses, pursue via `git_safety_guard-ct5s` (scenario definition) + extend `scripts/e2e_test.sh` to load them.
- For the canonical corpus + diffable regression harness used to prove isomorphism during perf refactors, see `git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4` (explicitly updated to build on `scripts/e2e_test.sh`).","created_at":"2026-01-09T21:20:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","title":"[PACK] payment.braintree - Braintree Pack","description":"# Braintree Payment Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Braintree/PayPal payment operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### API Operations
- Merchant account deletion
- Customer deletion: `DELETE /customers/{id}`
- Payment method deletion
- Subscription cancellation
- Webhook deletion

### SDK/CLI Patterns
- `braintree.Customer.delete()` in scripts
- `gateway.customer.delete()`

## Safe Patterns
- `braintree.Customer.find()`
- `gateway.customer.search()`
- Transaction queries
- GET API requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: payment.* (git_safety_guard-atw)
- Keywords: braintree
- Note: Often used via SDK in scripts

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for API DELETE patterns
- [ ] Unit tests for SDK delete method calls
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/payment_braintree.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Customer deletion blocked
- [ ] Merchant operations blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:10:51.737602532-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:38:08.268581097-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:38:08.268581097-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.579938797-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.620379618-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.663197801-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.706162379-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.749049071-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.796768306-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.312591122-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-m7f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.358562746-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mbq7","title":"[TASK] Documentation updates for new packs","description":"# Documentation Updates for New Packs

## Scope
Ensure all documentation is updated when new packs are added.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pack Listing in README
Update `README.md` with:
- Complete list of available packs by category
- Brief description of what each pack protects
- How to enable/disable specific packs

### 2. Pack Reference Documentation
Create `docs/packs/` directory with per-pack docs:
```
docs/packs/
├── README.md           # Index of all packs
├── secrets/
│   ├── vault.md       # Detailed Vault pack docs
│   ├── aws-secrets.md
│   └── ...
├── cicd/
│   └── ...
└── ...
```

Each pack doc includes:
- What commands are blocked (with examples)
- What commands are allowed (with examples)
- Configuration options
- Common false positive scenarios and how to handle

### 3. Configuration Guide
Create `docs/configuration.md`:
- How to enable/disable packs
- How to add custom allowlist entries
- How to configure pack behavior
- Environment variable reference

### 4. Troubleshooting Guide
Create `docs/troubleshooting.md`:
- Common issues and solutions
- How to debug false positives
- How to request new pack coverage
- How to report bugs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README lists all packs
- [ ] Each pack has reference documentation
- [ ] Configuration guide is complete
- [ ] Troubleshooting guide covers common issues
- [ ] Docs build without errors","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Add a doc consistency check (unit test or CI step) ensuring README pack list matches PackRegistry metadata.
- Run e2e harness to ensure documented examples still match actual output; log diffs if any.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:34:14.67283465-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:23:07.618686393-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:23:07.618686393-05:00","close_reason":"Updated README + docs/packs index to list all PackRegistry pack IDs; added doc consistency test. Per-pack reference docs tracked separately as git_safety_guard-m6ol."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","title":"Define JavaScript/Node destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define AST-aware matchers for JavaScript/Node heredoc bodies that detect **high-signal destructive operations** with minimal false positives.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on operations that are clearly destructive/irreversible.
- Do not turn this into a generic “JS security” scanner (avoid network/exfiltration heuristics).

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Recursive filesystem deletion (high signal)

- `fs.rmSync(path, { recursive: true, force: true })`
- `fs.rmSync(path, { recursive: true })`
- `fs.rmdirSync(path, { recursive: true })`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only for clearly catastrophic literal targets (e.g., `"/"` or other policy-defined high-risk literals).
- Otherwise `Warning` by default.

### 2) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- `child_process.execSync("rm -rf ...")` (literal)
- `spawnSync("rm", ["-rf", ...])` (literal)
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")` (literal)

Important:

- Do not flag `require('child_process')` alone.
- Do not flag `exec($VAR)` as a deny; it’s too common. At most warn if combined with other signals.

### 3) Known destructive package usage (optional / careful)

- `rimraf(...)` (common in builds) should likely be `Warning` unless literal catastrophic path.

## False positive mitigation

- Require negative fixtures that include common non-destructive uses:
  - `execSync("git status")`
  - `fs.rmSync("./dist", { recursive: true })` (should be warning at most)
  - code that merely prints dangerous strings

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a JS/Node pattern set that is high-signal and severity-tagged.
- Includes at least:
  - 5 positive fixtures (must match)
  - 5 negative fixtures (must not match)
- No matcher becomes a default hard deny unless it is clearly catastrophic.
- Explicitly documents which constructs are intentionally not matched to preserve trust.
","notes":"CrimsonFalcon: taking over to align severity (catastrophic literal path blocks; otherwise warn) + add missing JS/Ruby positive/negative fixtures; will run full gates and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:35:33.877595699-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.927767571-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.927767571-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented patterns + 5+ positive/negative test fixtures. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:35:39.990890431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:35:40.010899112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:25.768100887-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
